mini-project.label: Mini-Project: End-to-End Query
mini-project.summary: Combine your SQL skills to answer real business questions using the inventory_ops dataset.

total-value-active-inventory.title: Calculate Total Value of Active Inventory

most-recently-updated-active-item.title: Find the Most Recently Updated Active Item

intro-overview.title: Project Overview
intro-overview.bodyMarkdown: In this mini-project, you'll use the `inventory_items` table from the `inventory_ops` dataset. This table tracks items in inventory, including their name, category, price, status, and other details. You'll write queries to answer practical business questions, such as finding active products, calculating totals, and filtering out test or draft items.

table-schema.title: Table Schema and Sample Data
table-schema.bodyMarkdown:
The `inventory_items` table has the following columns:

- `id` (INTEGER): Unique identifier for each item
- `name` (TEXT): Name of the item
- `category` (TEXT): Item category (e.g., Accessories, Decor, Kitchen)
- `price` (REAL): Price of the item
- `status` (TEXT): Status of the item (e.g., active, draft, inactive)
- `is_test` (INTEGER): 1 if the item is a test/sample, 0 otherwise
- `notes` (TEXT): Optional notes
- `last_updated` (TEXT): Last update date

**Sample rows:**

| id | name           | category    | price | status   | is_test | notes              | last_updated |
|----|----------------|-------------|-------|----------|---------|--------------------|--------------|
| 1  | Sticker Pack   | Accessories | 4.99  | active   | 0       | NULL               | 2026-02-01   |
| 2  | Test Mug       | Kitchen     | 7.50  | draft    | 1       | Internal sample    | 2026-02-03   |
| 3  | Canvas Print   | Decor       | 45.00 | active   | 0       | NULL               | 2026-02-02   |

project-tasks.title: Project Tasks
project-tasks.bodyMarkdown:
You'll complete a series of queries to:

1. List all active, non-test items.
2. Calculate the total value of active inventory.
3. Find the most recently updated active item.

Each step builds on your SQL knowledge and helps answer a real business need.

list-active-non-test-items.title: List Active, Non-Test Inventory Items
list-active-non-test-items.prompt: Write a SQL query to select the `id`, `name`, and `category` of all items that are **active** and **not test items** (`is_test = 0`).
list-active-non-test-items.hint: Filter for status 'active' and is_test equals 0.

help.concept: Use the WHERE clause to filter rows based on multiple conditions. Combine conditions with AND.
help.hint_1: Check both the status and is_test columns in your WHERE clause.
help.hint_2: Remember to select only the requested columns and filter for items that are both active and not test items.

total-value-active-inventory.title: Calculate Total Value of Active Inventory
total-value-active-inventory.prompt: Write a SQL query to calculate the **total price** of all inventory items that are active and not test items.
total-value-active-inventory.hint: Use SUM on the price column, with the same filters as before.

help.concept: Aggregate functions like SUM can be used to total values across filtered rows.
help.hint_1: Apply SUM to the price column and use WHERE to filter for active, non-test items.
help.hint_2: Make sure your WHERE clause matches the previous filter, and use SUM(price) to get the total.

most-recently-updated-active-item.title: Find the Most Recently Updated Active Item
most-recently-updated-active-item.prompt: Write a SQL query to select the `id`, `name`, and `last_updated` of the **active, non-test** item that was updated most recently.
most-recently-updated-active-item.hint: Order by last_updated in descending order and limit the result.

help.concept: ORDER BY sorts results; LIMIT restricts the number of rows returned.
help.hint_1: Filter for active, non-test items, then order by last_updated descending.
help.hint_2: Use LIMIT to return only the most recent item after sorting.
